growth_records.medium: mPGM (modified Postgate's medium; PGM + 25 mM glucose)

up.method: HPLC-RI; D. piger monoculture consumed lactate; numeric value only in Fig. 1f, not in text
HPLC-RI; text states D. piger monoculture consumed lactate 'without consuming glucose' (non-fermenting) -> glucose uptake ~0
D. piger is a sulfate-reducing bacterium (Na2SO4 1 g/L + MgSO4 in medium); sulfate reduction implied but not quantified

sec.method: HPLC-RI/GC; D. piger monoculture produced acetate; numeric value only in Fig. 1f
implied product of sulfate reduction; not quantified in this in vitro experiment

growth_records.cite: Khan et al. Synergy and oxygen adaptation for development of next-generation probiotics. Nature (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41586-023-06378-w. [PMC10412450]
growth_records.snippet: monocultures of D. piger consumed lactate and produced acetate without consuming glucose ... D. piger functioned as an electron sink in the co-culture in PGM as it consumed lactate; D. piger generated acetate that was used by F. prausnitzii
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic monoculture in defined mPGM. D. piger is an obligate anaerobic non-fermenting sulfate reducer using lactate as electron donor/carbon source (sodium lactate 3.5 g/L in PGM base). Text explicitly reports direction: lactate consumed, acetate produced, glucose NOT consumed (glucose uptake set to 0). No growth rate/doubling time reported (growth not shown numerically for D. piger). Metabolite magnitudes are in Fig. 1f only, not in text -> qS/qP not computable without fabrication; left null. Sulfate reduction and H2S production are implied by organism physiology and medium sulfate content but were not quantified here (faecal H2S measured in vivo elsewhere, not a rate).
